title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Stateless Application State
summary: A fleet-control CLI calls an inventory service through a cache and gateway, but operators cannot tell whether failures come from naming, transport, status, or decoding. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go stateless application state increment in a Go 1.26 net/http protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain protocol statelessness while locating cookies, tokens, caches, and workflow state outside core request semantics. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Trace request construction, routing, transport, intermediary handling, response interpretation, and cleanup as distinct stages. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
